name: Personal Data Protection Law No. 151 of 2020
summary: Egypt's first comprehensive data protection law regulates the processing of personal data by both public and private entities. The Executive Regulations were issued in November 2025 with a 12-month grace period, meaning full enforcement begins approximately November 2026.
keyRequirements: Obtain explicit consent before processing personal data
Obtain a license or permit from the Personal Data Protection Center
Notify the PDPC of data breaches within 72 hours, and affected individuals within 3 working days
Conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
Restrict cross-border transfers to PDPC-approved countries with data subject consent
penalties: Imprisonment of 3-6 months and fines from EGP 200,000 to EGP 5,000,000 depending on severity. Unauthorized cross-border transfer: minimum 3 months imprisonment and/or EGP 500,000-5,000,000 fine. Enforced by the Personal Data Protection Center (under Ministry of Communications and Information Technology).
